# {{project_name}}

LangGraph application generated by arch2code from AIR run `{{run_id}}`.

The deployable surface is `langgraph.json`: it points `graphs.{{graph_id}}` at
`src/graphs/{{entry_component_id}}.py:graph`, the module-level **compiled** graph.

## Run order

```bash
# 1. Install the project (dependencies: ["."] in the manifest installs it).
pip install -e .

# 2. Set the environment. NO keys live in code — everything is read at runtime.
export LLM_PROVIDER=groq                 # architectural default
export LLM_MODEL=openai/gpt-oss-120b     # NOT a legacy watsonx granite id
export GROQ_API_KEY=...                   # provider key (init_chat_model reads it)
# export LLM_FALLBACKS="openai:gpt-4o-mini"   # optional backups for the router
export CHECKPOINTER=memory               # memory | sqlite | postgres | none
# export CHECKPOINTER_DB_URI=postgresql://...  # required for sqlite/postgres
# export OBSERVABILITY=langsmith         # none | langsmith | langfuse
# export LANGCHAIN_TRACING_V2=true; export LANGCHAIN_API_KEY=...

# 3. Run the graph locally.
langgraph dev            # hot-reloading dev server
# or invoke it directly: python -c "from src.graphs.{{entry_component_id}} import graph; print(graph.invoke({'messages': []}))"

# 4. Validate the manifest (this is the real gate; needs langgraph-cli installed).
langgraph build -t {{project_slug}}
```

## AIR-to-graph mapping

The topology below is how this profile turned the drawing into a graph. Check it
against the drawing — a node that should have been an edge, or a database drawn as
a node, RUNS and is WRONG.

| Drawing (AIR kind) | Becomes | Where |
|---|---|---|
| service | a **node** (state -> partial state) | `src/nodes/{id}.py` |
| gateway | a **conditional edge** (route fn + mapping) | `src/nodes/{id}.py` |
| agent (specialist) | a `create_react_agent` **subgraph** | `src/graphs/{id}.py` |
| agent (supervisor) | a **router StateGraph** over the subgraphs | `src/graphs/{id}.py` |
| boundary | ONE compiled **subgraph** added as a node | `src/graphs/{id}.py` |
| tool | a `@tool` **function** (react loop calls it) | `src/tools/{id}.py` |
| knowledge_base | a **retriever tool** (not a node) | `src/tools/{id}_retriever.py` |
| database | the **checkpointer** (compile arg, NOT a node) | `src/checkpointer.py` |
| cache | the **store** (compile arg, NOT a node) | `src/checkpointer.py` |
| plugin-pre / guardrails / PII | a wrapper **node before** the model | `src/guardrails_pre.py` |
| redaction / plugin-post | a wrapper **node after** the model | `src/redaction_post.py` |
| actor (human-in-the-loop) | `interrupt_before=[node]` at compile() | `src/graphs/{id}.py` |
| LLM box / multi-LLM gateway | `get_model()` (init_chat_model + fallbacks) | `llm/router.py` |
| LLM-Judge | an **evaluator over traces**, not a node | `eval/observability.py`, `eval/ragas/` |
| governance / monitoring | a loud placeholder client | `governance/openscale_monitor.py` |

## What this profile refuses

A UI, a broker (queue/topic), a schedule and object storage are **not** graph
concepts here — see `capabilities.*.excludes` in the profile for the redraw or
the target swap that makes each one work. A database is a checkpointer, never a
node; that is the single mapping mistake that turns a correct drawing into a graph
that reasons over its own persistence.

## Traceability

Every generated module carries a `# arch2code: generated from AIR {{run_id}} ::
<component_id>` header. `langgraph.json` carries none (JSON forbids comments); its
provenance lives in this file and in the modules it points at.
